items.hook: Ten, Nine, Eight is a children's picture book by Molly Bang, published in 1983 by Greenwillow Books.
items.body: It is a countdown from ten to one by a little girl who is getting ready for bed. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.opener: Here's one for the group chat —
items.hook: The Ring and the Book is a long dramatic narrative poem, and, more specifically, a verse novel, of 21,000 lines, written by Robert Browning.
items.body: It was published in four volumes from 1868 to 1869 by Smith, Elder & Co. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Prosecutors argue D4vd had financial motivation to kill Celeste Rivas Hernandez
items.body:
Details emerge about how much Interscope Records paid the singer at hearing to determine if there is probable cause for murder case to proceed to trial

items.hook: The second Blue Water Bridge opens between Port Huron, Michigan and Sarnia, Ontario.
items.body: The Blue Water Bridge is a twin-span international bridge across the St. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: The Acts of Union 1707 are agreed upon by commissioners from the Kingdom of England and the Kingdom of Scotland, which, when passed by each country's Parliament, leads to the creation of the Kingdom of Great Britain.
items.body: The Acts of Union refer to two acts of Parliament, one by the Parliament of Scotland in March 1707, followed shortly thereafter by an equivalent act of the Parliament of England. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: The Red Frenzy or The Red Rage is a 1962 West German thriller film directed by Wolfgang Schleif and starring Klaus Kinski, Brigitte Grothum and Marina Petrova.
items.body: It was shot at the Kalvarienberg Studios in Vienna. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: The Naked Sun is a science fiction novel by American writer Isaac Asimov, the second in his Robot series.
items.body: Like its predecessor, The Caves of Steel, this is a whodunit story. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Charles Gaines, whose book Pumping Iron helped to popularize bodybuilding, dies at 84
items.body:
1974 book led to making of seminal documentary and introduced Arnold Schwarzenegger to the world

Charles Gaines, the author whose book and documentary Pumping Iron helped bring professional bodybuilding into the mainstream and intr

items.hook: Abdur Rahman is proclaimed emir of Afghanistan after having been accepted as ruler by the British.
items.body: Abdur Rahman Khan Barakzai, also known by his epithet as the Iron Emir, was Emir of Afghanistan from 11 August 1880 until his death on 1 October 1901. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Toronto’s expanded Platform section unveils 12 world premieres
items.body: The festival’s only competitive section offers a cash prize and potential Oscar eligibility.

items.hook: EU approves Paramount’s takeover of Warner Bros Discovery with conditions
items.body: Paramount agrees to end United International Pictures (UIP) film distribution joint venture with Universal Pictures.

items.hook: Why open-source software can be both good and bad
items.body: Software that is free to use creates many opportunities – not all of which are good

items.hook: The 100 best novels of all time
items.body: The top 100 novels of all time published in English, as voted for by authors, critics and academics worldwide. How many have you read?

items.hook: Mariner program: Mariner 1 spacecraft flies erratically several minutes after launch and has to be destroyed.
items.body: The Mariner program was conducted by the American space agency NASA to explore other planets. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Spanish Civil War: The Popular Executive Committee of Valencia takes power in the Valencian Community.
items.body: The Spanish Civil War was fought from 1936 to 1939 between the Republicans and the Nationalist rebels. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Georgian organisations claim state festivals are being hijacked by “authoritarian regime”
items.body: Top Georgian groups say international guests are being used as “useful idiots”.

items.hook: Beaver Towers: The Dark Dream is a 1997 novel by British author Nigel Hinton.
items.body: It is the fourth and final installment in the Beaver Towers series. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Crown Colony of Sarawak gains self-governance.
items.body: The Crown Colony of Sarawak was a British Crown colony on the island of Borneo, established in 1946, shortly after the dissolution of the British Military Administration. The world is stranger than it lets on.
